header.note: Layer 1 (deterministic) is patch-extraction recall and replays byte-identical from the committed corpora. Layer 2 (executed) re-runs real test suites in provisioned workspaces; its per-PR proof envelopes are committed next to each result.json. Funnel and live verdicts are from an actual executed run of the 8+1 surviving PRs (some with 49 qualifying findings); some PRs hit runner-unsupported or no-test-hunks (correct per engine). 0 executed false proofs on clean.

oracle.definitionOfTargets: non-null patch whose parsed chunk at the label hunkIndex starts at the label startLine

misses.classification: evidence limit, correct behavior: the injected hunk lives in a source file, not a test file, so there are no tampered test hunks to restore and the engine correctly extracts nothing (a restoration would revert production code, which is not what the proof reverts)

clean.note: reaching would-execute is not a false positive: execution arbitrates, and a clean restored test passing yields `refuted`, which demotes the finding. A static clean case cannot produce a false proof by construction, because `proven` requires two executed failing restored runs plus two passing executed controls and the static layer executes nothing. Run through the record path without a sandbox, every static case lands in `not-proven:no-workspace`.
